scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented REST module/read/write endpoint reachable from the standard OAuth refresh-token grant this bundle's AuthHook implements. Bulk API, Notification (webhook) API, and Query API (the 3 other API families the v2 landing page names alongside REST) are out of scope -- see exclusions below for why each family specifically doesn't fit this dialect.

excluded.reason: single-record fetch by id is a strict subset of the records/contacts/companies/etc. list streams already covered; no dedicated single-record stream is needed since the engine's declarative read path always lists, never fetches-by-id

related-list traversal needs a per-parent-record fan_out (docs/migration/conventions.md's fan_out dialect) whose id source would itself be one of the already-covered list streams (contacts/companies/pipelines); a dedicated per-relation stream per module pairing is combinatorial (Contacts->Notes, Companies->Tasks, Pipelines->Products, etc.) and not requested by any known consumer of this bundle -- Notes is already covered as its own top-level module-scoped stream (GET /Notes), which is the one related-list relation with real read demand

criteria/email/phone/word search is a query-shaped read (parameterized filter, not a syncable list), out of scope for this bundle's declarative full-table stream model; the existing list streams already page through every record

attachment listing returns file metadata but the actual file/photo download (GET .../Attachments/{id}) and upload (POST multipart/form-data) are binary payloads the JSON declarative dialect cannot express; a metadata-only list stream with no way to fetch the underlying binary has limited standalone value and was not prioritized for this pass
returns raw image bytes, not JSON; the dialect has no binary-response stream type
multipart/form-data file upload; the write dialect's body_type is json|form|none, none of which express a multipart file part
multipart/form-data image upload; same limitation as record attachment upload
deleting a specific attachment is only meaningful paired with the attachment listing/upload surface this pass excludes as binary_payload; no read stream exists to source attachment_id from
paired with the excluded photo upload/download surface
related-list layout metadata (which related lists exist per module), not itself a syncable record stream; supports the already-excluded related-list read surface
page-layout/field-arrangement UI metadata, not syncable business data

sub-pipeline/stage configuration metadata for the Pipelines module; the pipelines stream (GET /Pipelines) already covers the pipeline records themselves, and this settings variant is the same information from the metadata/configuration side, not additional record data
organization currency configuration (symbol, decimal places, exchange rate), not a syncable business-record stream
user role/hierarchy metadata; no read demand identified for this pass beyond the users stream's own embedded role/profile lookup objects
permission-profile metadata; same reasoning as /settings/roles
sales-territory hierarchy metadata; no read demand identified for this pass
creating a new tag definition (distinct from tagging a record) is a settings/admin-shaped mutation with low sync-pipeline demand relative to the record CRUD + notes surface prioritized this pass
renaming an existing tag definition; same reasoning as tag creation
deleting a tag definition; same reasoning as tag creation
associates existing tags with one or more records (a many-to-many join mutation on the tags stream, not a record CRUD action); lower priority than the core record/notes write surface for this pass
removes tag associations from one or more records; same reasoning as add_tags

note content update; create_note/delete_note cover the note write lifecycle's create and destroy ends, which is the pairing with the most sync-pipeline demand (append an activity note, retract one); update was not prioritized this pass
single-user fetch by id is a strict subset of the users stream (GET /users) already covered

creating a Bigin user is an organization-administration action requiring an admin-tier OAuth scope beyond the standard record-CRUD scopes this bundle's refresh-token grant is documented/tested against
modifying a user's role/profile/status is an organization-administration action, same elevated-scope reasoning as user creation
deactivating/deleting an organizational user account is an irreversible administration action outside this bundle's record-sync purpose
organization metadata snapshot (company profile, license, feature flags), not a syncable object stream

the separate Bulk Read/Write API (CSV-oriented, async job-submit-then-poll-then-download-a-file protocol) is architecturally distinct from this bundle's synchronous per-page REST reads; large-volume sync is already served by this bundle's ordinary paginated list streams for Bigin's realistic per-org record volumes (a small-business CRM, not a large-record-count system) -- if a genuine need for async bulk export/import surfaces, it is a StreamHook/WriteHook (Tier-2) candidate, not a Tier-1 addition, since it needs job-poll-then-download orchestration this dialect cannot express

the Notifications (webhook subscription) API registers Bigin-initiated push callbacks to a consumer-hosted URL; this is an inbound-webhook-receiver concern for the connector's OWN operator infrastructure, not a pollable read/write surface this connector's outbound HTTP dialect can express

the CRM Object Query Language (COQL) API accepts an arbitrary SQL-like query string in a POST body and is a power-user ad hoc query surface, not a fixed syncable stream shape; every record set it could return is already reachable via this bundle's ordinary list streams
